# Detailed Description of Figure 8

## Figure Type
This is a geologic index map showing a permissive mineral resource tract and prospect locations overlaid on a geographic base map with an inset location map.

## Coverage and Subject
The map covers the Teniz Basin in northern Kazakhstan, spanning approximately 67°E to 71°E longitude and 50°N to 52°N latitude. The assessed tract is designated **142ssCu8050 (TZ-1)**, shown as a large shaded polygon covering much of central and northern Kazakhstan. A prominent water body, **Lake Tengiz**, is visible in the south-central portion of the map near 50°N, 69°E. The city of **Astana** is marked in the eastern portion at approximately 51°N, 71°E. The map uses the Asia North Albers Equal Area Conic Projection with central meridian at 69°E and latitude of origin at 30°N.

## Key Content
The **assessed sandstone copper tract 142ssCu8050** is shown in solid salmon-pink/coral shading and covers an irregular, approximately 400 km (north-south) by 300 km (east-west) area. The tract comprises several disconnected polygons, with the largest body dominating the central and northern regions, and smaller outlying polygons to the west and southeast.

Two categories of copper prospects are mapped:
- **Sandstone copper significant prospects** (shown as solid black squares with white borders)
- **Sandstone copper prospects** (shown as open black squares)

Numerous prospect locations are labeled with names including (from north to south, west to east): Zholboldy, Valimirkovskoe, Belorodskoe, Doroginskoe, Konyr, Krasnopolyanskoe, Katynikovskoe, Irivakovskoe, Chizhi-1, Dolinasay, Drill-hole P-205, Borisovskoe, Sovetskoe, Darbassar, Kirubaykul, Akimovskoe, Smirnoskoe, Krymnikael, Uvynkov, Kumikael, Kiellysay, Nagaibay-1, Koksaytal, Taslyadeyr, Krymibay, Area No.8, Loozovskoe, Davka-Kalgan, Sarkala, Otta-Kargal, Drill-hole 138, Uzhkarasu, Szyt-Oba, Shyom, Aksa-2, Torguatbatskoe, Aksu, Kenen, Medan, Aynaly, Sharobr west, Tanalytumdar, Korokrazan, Sharclyn, Barolen, Lake Tengiz, Balashapstoe, Kedondsal, Kokaralnas, Kulun Iltkes, Byktumbay, and others.

A small **inset map** in the lower right shows the location of the study area (boxed) within Kazakhstan and adjacent Russia, at approximately 50-55°N latitude.

## Text and Labels

**Title/Caption:** 
"Figure 8. Map showing location of permissive tract 142ssCu8050 (TZ-1) and known prospects, Teniz Basin, Kazakhstan. Detailed descriptive information is available in both the GIS database and in tables 2 and 3."

**Map annotations:**
- Longitude labels: 67° E, 69° E, 71° E
- Latitude labels: 50° N, 51° N, 52° N
- Scale bars: "0 30 60 90 120 KILOMETERS" and "0 30 60 MILES"
- "Political boundaries from U.S. Department of State (2009)"
- "Asia North Albers Equal Area Conic Projection"
- "Central meridian, 69° E., latitude of origin, 30° N."
- "Lake Tengiz" (water body label)
- "Astana" (city)

**Inset map labels:**
- "RUSSIA"
- "KAZAKHSTAN"
- Coordinates: 60° E, 70° E, 80° E, 50° N, 55° N

**Explanation/Legend:**
"EXPLANATION
Sediment-hosted sandstone copper
- Assessed sandstone copper tract 142ssCu8050 [shown in coral/pink shading]
- Sandstone copper significant prospect [solid black square symbol]
- Sandstone copper prospect [open square symbol]"

**Page header:** "Development of the Prospects Database    21"

## Research Value
This figure provides the fundamental spatial framework for the Teniz Basin sandstone copper assessment by delineating the extent of permissive tract TZ-1 (142ssCu8050) and documenting the distribution of over 60 known copper prospects and significant prospects within and near the tract boundaries. The dense clustering of prospects in the northern and western portions of the tract, particularly around 52°N, 68-70°E, suggests areas of highest known mineralization and likely guides the statistical assessment of undiscovered resources. The map enables geologists to understand the geographic scope of prospective geology, assess spatial patterns in copper occurrences relative to structural or stratigraphic controls, and identify data gaps where exploration may be warranted.
